subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d and f Block Elements

questions.q: Explain the following: (i) Atomic size of lanthanides (ii) Oxidation state of transition elements (iii) Formation of interstitial compounds
questions.answer: (i) The atomic size of lanthanides decreases from La to Lu. This decrease in size is known as lanthanide contraction. (ii) Transition elements exhibit variable oxidation states due to the incomplete filling of d orbitals. (iii) Transition elements can trap atoms of other elements in the interstitial sites of their crystal lattices, forming interstitial compounds.
marking_scheme: Explanation of atomic size of lanthanides (1 mark)
Explanation of oxidation state of transition elements (1.5 marks)
Explanation of formation of interstitial compounds (2.5 marks)
questions.topic: General Properties of the d and f Block Elements

questions.q: Calculate the number of unpaired electrons in the ground state of Mn2+ ion. (At. no. of Mn = 25)
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Mn (At. no. = 25) : [Ar] 3d5 4s2 
Mn2+ : [Ar] 3d5 
Number of unpaired electrons = 5
marking_scheme: Correct electronic configuration of Mn (1 mark)
Correct electronic configuration of Mn2+ (1 mark)
questions.topic: Electronic Configuration of d Block Elements

questions.q: Discuss the following: (i) Differences between lanthanides and actinides (ii) Applications of lanthanides
questions.answer: (i) Lanthanides are non-radioactive, possess only +3 oxidation state, and have less tendency to form complexes. Actinides are radioactive, exhibit variable oxidation states, and have more tendency to form complexes. (ii) Lanthanides are used in magnets, phosphors, and lasers.
marking_scheme: Differences between lanthanides and actinides (2.5 marks)

questions.q: The ionisation enthalpy of Cu is higher than that of Ni. Explain.
questions.answer:
Calculation: Not required. 
Explanation: The ionisation enthalpy of Cu is higher than that of Ni due to the stable d10 configuration of Cu, which makes it more difficult to remove an electron.
marking_scheme: Stable d10 configuration of Cu (2 marks)
questions.topic: General Properties of the d and f Block Elements

questions.q: Calculate the spin-only magnetic moment of Ti3+ ion. (At. no. of Ti = 22)
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Ti (At. no. = 22) : [Ar] 3d2 4s2 
Ti3+ : [Ar] 3d1 
Number of unpaired electrons = 1 
Spin-only magnetic moment (μ) = √[n(n+2)] BM = √[1(1+2)] BM = √3 BM
marking_scheme: Correct electronic configuration of Ti (1 mark)
Correct electronic configuration of Ti3+ (1 mark)
Calculation of spin-only magnetic moment (2 marks)

questions.q: Calculate the number of unpaired electrons in the ground state of Cr2+ ion. (At. no. of Cr = 24)
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Cr (At. no. = 24) : [Ar] 3d5 4s1 
Cr2+ : [Ar] 3d4 
Number of unpaired electrons = 4
marking_scheme: Correct electronic configuration of Cr (1 mark)
Correct electronic configuration of Cr2+ (1 mark)
questions.topic: Electronic Configuration of d Block Elements
